text.title: App Store and Play Store Release Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the App Store and Play Store Release methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One release version per playbook run; batching v2.3.0 and v2.4.0 hides per-version regression signal.
rule.rationale: Per-version isolation is required to attribute crash-rate / rating delta to the right release.

rule.statement: Releases >100 active users MUST go through staged rollout 1% → 10% → 50% → 100% with explicit gate criteria.

rationale.source: Apple App Store guidance + Google Play staged rollout docs

rule.rationale: Direct-to-100% releases lose the cheap rollback window; staged rollout is the only way to bound incident blast radius.

rule.statement: Gate criteria for each rollout stage MUST be numerically expressed (crash rate ≤ baseline + 0.5pp, rating delta ≥ -0.1).
rule.rationale: Vague 'looks fine' criteria invite optimistic continuation; numeric criteria force the rollback decision.

rule.statement: Reviewer notes MUST cover every non-trivial new feature with a reproduction path; missing notes are the top reviewer-rejection cause.
rule.rationale: App Store reviewers reject features they cannot reproduce; cheap to provide notes, expensive to fight rejection.

rule.statement: Crash rate, store rating, support tickets MUST be watched for 72h post-launch; rollback decision authority is named.
rule.rationale: Without a named decision authority, rollback discussions stall while the regression spreads.
